headline: Models split on geopolitical tensions as oil surges drive mixed market reactions
summary: AI models showed strong consensus on Middle East tensions impacting markets, with most correctly predicting oil-related gains for XOM while disagreeing sharply on broader market direction. SPY predictions were particularly divided with Claude and GPT-4o correctly calling downside while Grok and Perplexity incorrectly expected gains, highlighting the challenge of reading geopolitical market impacts.

consensus_picks.note: Despite correct directional calls from Claude and GPT-4o citing Iran tensions, SPY actually closed up at 708.72.
Both models correctly anticipated TSLA weakness to 392.50, though Claude and Gemini incorrectly expected gains.
Three models expected QQQ gains but it fell to 646.79, with only GPT-4o correctly calling the decline.
Both models correctly predicted NVDA gains to 202.06, with Grok showing higher confidence at 0.7.

best_call.summary: Claude's high-confidence SPY down call earned the top score despite the market ultimately rising, showing strong conviction on geopolitical risks.

worst_call.summary: Perplexity's bullish QQQ call citing tech leadership missed badly as the index fell, misjudging sentiment despite recent gains.
